sourceMovie.title: Dragonfly
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Dragonfly works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Horns
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Dragonfly clicked for you, Horns (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Ghost
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Dragonfly clicked for you, Ghost (1990) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and fantasy, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Dragonfly?
faq.answer: Try Horns, Ghost, The Killing of a Sacred Deer—each shares DNA with Dragonfly while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Dragonfly?
faq.answer: Dragonfly (2002) is best described as Drama, Thriller, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Dragonfly worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
